area: FIVE NOMINATIONS (ranked): (1) 93D25/15A60/90C22 — switched-system stability, constrained joint spectral radius, path-complete Lyapunov certificates; (2) 03C60/11U09 — model theory of p-adic fields, definable invariant sets (Macintyre/Denef); (3) 37K60/17B37/68Q80 — ultradiscrete integrable systems, box-ball solitons, crystal-base combinatorics; (4) 37C50/65G — shadowing theory and rigorous computer-assisted dynamics; (5) 91A46 — combinatorial game theory (subtraction-division games, loopy/misère theory)
hole_justification: Per-candidate, with named searches. (1) CONTROL/JSR — fringe: searched ["Collatz joint spectral radius switched system"], ["Collatz 3x+1 switched linear system stability control theory"], ["Blondel Tsitsiklis 3x+1 Collatz undecidability survey"], ["Ahmadi Jungers Parrilo path-complete arXiv"]. Found exactly two fringe items: Hung-style 'Analogy Between the Collatz Conjecture and Sliding Mode Control' (2021, qualitative, no theorems) and Leventides–Poulios 'An operator theoretic approach to the 3x+1 dynamical system' (IFAC 2021, Koopman-flavored; already in our papers dir). Blondel–Tsitsiklis's complexity surveys cite the 3x+1 problem as motivation but NOBODY has computed a constrained JSR for the Collatz pair or attempted path-complete/SOS Lyapunov certificates — zero hits for 'Collatz path-complete' and 'Collatz constrained joint spectral radius'. (2) P-ADIC MODEL THEORY — virgin: searched ["Collatz p-adic semialgebraic subanalytic cell decomposition definable invariant Macintyre"], ["Monks 3x+1 semilinear invariant sets generalization p-adic definable"]. Nothing beyond Monks 2006 (Presburger case) and the brand-new 2026 Büchi-arithmetic thread (arXiv 2601.12772, 2602.06066) which is the base-2-automatic analogue and falls under our existing 03C Cobham–Semenov coverage; the Macintyre/Denef semialgebraic side is untouched. (3) BOX-BALL — virgin on the integrable side, fringe on the CA side: searched ["box-ball OR ultradiscrete Collatz 3x+1"] — zero hits connecting them; ["Kari cellular automata Collatz powers of 3/2 Kopra"] confirms the only nearby work is Kari (DLT 2012) and Kari–Kopra (RAIRO 2017), which never invoke solitons, conserved quantities, or crystals. (4) SHADOWING — virgin: searched ["Collatz 3x+1 shadowing property pseudo-orbit dynamical systems"] — zero relevant hits; shadowing literature never mentions Collatz despite T|Z_2 being conjugate to the 2-shift (which has POTP). (5) GAMES — virgin academically: searched ["Collatz combinatorial game Sprague-Grundy misère P-positions"], ["Collatz game arXiv impartial"] — only a 2010 hobbyist blog post (combinatorialgametheory.blogspot.com) and 'Collatz convergence is a Hydra game' (arXiv 2101.09719), which is ordinal/termination-flavored and belongs to our covered 06A/03F lane; Fraenkel's Mark-game work (the natural adjacent theory) never touches ×3+1 moves. Overall enum forced to one value: 'fringe' is the honest mode (candidates 2,3-integrable,4,5 are virgin; 1 and 3-CA have 1-3 isolated papers each).
bridge: (1) CONTROL: The accelerated Collatz map is literally a state-feedback switched affine system: on the homogeneous lift x=(n,1), x_{k+1}=A_{σ(k)}x_k with A_even=[[1/2,0],[0,1]], A_odd=[[3/2,1/2],[0,1]], where σ is not free but determined by parity feedback. A divergent orbit = an unbounded trajectory of the feedback-constrained system; the conjecture's growth half says every integer-realizable switching signal has asymptotic odd-density ≤ log2/log3. The theory of the CONSTRAINED joint spectral radius (switching restricted by an automaton; Philippe et al. 2016) is exactly 'growth under ω-regular switching constraints', and path-complete graph Lyapunov functions (AJPR 2014) are automaton-indexed families of norms certifying stability — i.e., a REGULAR CONVERGENCE CERTIFICATE, the precise dual of our just-completed regular DIVERGENCE certificate no-go (≤7 DFA states). Yolcu–Aaronson–Heule's matrix interpretations are the linear-ranking special case of this in rewriting clothing. Blondel–Tsitsiklis undecidability of matrix-product boundedness is the control-side twin of Conway/Kurtz–Simon. Known obstruction the bridge must respect: at every finite modulus 2^k the parity-constraint automaton is full (every parity word admissible), and the 2-adic cycles through -1,-5,-17 make the naive constrained JSR equal 3/2 at every finite level — so certificates must use the affine (+1) term and positivity (cone-constrained JSR, Protasov), which is where the new mathematics lives. (2) P-ADIC MODEL THEORY: Monks 2006 says no infinite T-invariant SEMILINEAR (=Presburger-definable) subset of Z+ avoids {1,2}. T on Z_2 is semialgebraic in Macintyre's language (parity = valuation condition, branches polynomial). The bridge: climb the definability hierarchy Presburger → Büchi-arithmetic (being done right now by others, 2026) → p-adic semialgebraic → subanalytic, using Denef cell decomposition: every semialgebraic S ⊆ Z_2 is a finite union of cells fibered over congruence data, so T-invariance should collapse cells to the congruence case Monks already handled, plus finitely many algebraic exceptional points. Objects: definable sets ↔ candidate structured divergent sets; cell decomposition ↔ reduction to arithmetic progressions; QE ↔ effective classification of invariant sets. (3) BOX-BALL: Kari's reversible CA F_×3 in base 6 simulates Collatz steps; multiplication automata are piecewise-linear local rules on digit strings, formally the same (max,+)/(min,+) piecewise-linear class as the box-ball system, which is ultradiscrete KdV — an INTEGRABLE cellular automaton whose solitons and conserved quantities are fully classified and whose dynamics is linearized by the Kerov–Kirillov–Reshetikhin rigged-configuration bijection (crystal bases of U_q(sl2-hat)). Correspondence: ball configurations ↔ digit/carry streams of the base-6 Collatz tape; soliton conserved quantities ↔ orbit invariants of T; KKR linearization ↔ hoped-for change of coordinates making carry propagation free dynamics. Any nontrivial local conserved quantity of the Collatz-CA is a new invariant of Collatz orbits — an object the field has literally none of. (4) SHADOWING: T|Z_2 conjugate to the full 2-shift ⇒ T has the pseudo-orbit tracing property with computable modulus. Every finite/mod-2^k Collatz computation (including our certificate searches) is a pseudo-orbit; shadowing converts finite-precision residue computations into exact statements about genuine 2-adic orbits nearby. The conjecture = 'divergent-looking pseudo-orbits are only ever shadowed by non-integer 2-adic points'; quantifying WHICH points shadow (Lipschitz shadowing ⇔ structural stability, Pilyugin–Tikhomirov) turns the integer/2-adic mismatch into a measurable quantity. (5) GAMES: Fraenkel–Guo's Mark game (moves n→n-1, n→⌊n/2⌋) has an exactly computed Sprague–Grundy function given by binary digit criteria (odious/evil, trailing zeros) — proof that division-move games can have automatic SG functions. The inverse-Collatz game (moves n→2n, n→(2n-1)/3 when defined, capped arena; loopy-game theory à la Siegel for the cycles) puts a game-value sheaf on the backward Collatz tree; P-position structure ↔ arithmetic of the tree; automaticity or provable non-automaticity of the P-set plugs directly into our automata program.
strongest_adjacent_theorems: Blondel–Tsitsiklis (Systems & Control Letters 41, 2000): boundedness of the set of all products of a pair of rational matrices is undecidable, and 'JSR ≤ 1' is undecidable — the switched-systems twin of Conway 1972/Kurtz–Simon 2007.

Ahmadi–Jungers–Parrilo–Roozbehani (SIAM J. Control Optim. 52(1), 2014, arXiv:1111.3427): path-complete graph Lyapunov functions give SDP hierarchies approximating the JSR with proven accuracy ρ ≤ ρ_est ≤ ρ·n^(1/2k), unifying common-quadratic, min/max-of-quadratics and SOS certificates; certificates are indexed by automata over the switching alphabet.

Philippe–Essick–Dullerud–Jungers (Automatica 2016, arXiv:1503.06984): the constrained joint spectral radius under automaton-restricted switching is approximable to arbitrary accuracy via multinorms (one norm per automaton state) — exactly 'growth rate under regular switching constraints'.
Bousch–Mairesse (J. AMS 15, 2002): the Lagarias–Wang finiteness conjecture is FALSE — a caution that optimal switching growth need not be attained periodically.

Macintyre (1976) quantifier elimination for Q_p + Denef (1984/86) cell decomposition: every p-adic semialgebraic set is a finite union of cells with congruence-class fibers, and definable Poincaré series are rational — the engine for classifying T-invariant definable sets, one rung above Monks 2006 (Presburger) and the 2026 Büchi-arithmetic non-definability papers.

Tokihiro–Takahashi–Matsukidaira–Satsuma (Phys. Rev. Lett. 76, 1996): ultradiscretization sends KdV to the box-ball system, whose soliton content and conserved quantities survive the tropical limit; Kuniba–Okado–Sakamoto–Takagi–Yamada et al.: the KKR rigged-configuration bijection (crystal-base combinatorics) LINEARIZES box-ball dynamics — action-angle variables for a piecewise-linear automaton.

Kari–Kopra (RAIRO-ITA 51, 2017, arXiv:1710.05737): theorems on distribution of fractional parts of ξ(p/q)^n proved via the multiplication CA F_{p/q} in base pq, tying the automaton to Mahler's Z-number problem — the nearest real result to a 'Collatz-CA invariant'.

Anosov–Bowen shadowing lemma / Walters (1978): shifts of finite type (hence T on Z_2, conjugate to the 2-shift) have the pseudo-orbit tracing property; Pilyugin–Tikhomirov (2010): Lipschitz shadowing is equivalent to structural stability — quantitative rigidity for who shadows whom.

Fraenkel (Electron. J. Combin. 18(2), 2011 'Aperiodic subtraction games') and Fraenkel (Discrete Math. 312, 2012 'The vile, dopey, evil and odious game players'): exact Sprague–Grundy function of the subtraction-division game Mark (n→n-1 or ⌊n/2⌋) via binary digit statistics — SG functions of division-move games can be automatic without being periodic.
the_push: (1) CONTROL (sharpest): Reframe 'no divergent trajectories' as cone-constrained stability of the affine switched system and attack it with the mature certificate machinery: multinorms on the mod-2^k de Bruijn lift + SOS polynomial Lyapunov functions valid on the positive cone, where the affine +1 term (negligible at infinity but sign-coherent only on positives) is precisely what distinguishes N from the 2-adic cycles through -1,-5,-17 that kill all homogeneous finite-level certificates. Best case: a finite path-complete/SOS certificate that no positive divergent orbit exists — that would split the conjecture (divergence excluded, only finitely-checkable cycles remain, complementing Hercher). Structurally-likely case: a proven no-go 'no degree-d SOS multinorm certificate on the mod-2^k lift exists for all (d,k) ≤ (D,K)' — a strict, publishable extension of our 7-state DFA theorem from Boolean to analytic/weighted certificates, mapping exactly where the certificate wall sits. Either outcome advances the program; the tools (JSR toolbox, SwitchOnSafety.jl, Mosek) are off-the-shelf. (2) P-ADIC MODEL THEORY: push Monks 2006 up the hierarchy: 'no infinite T-invariant p-adic-semialgebraic subset of Z+ avoids {1,2}' via cell decomposition reducing invariant semialgebraic sets to congruence cores + finitely many algebraic points. This complements the live 2026 Büchi-arithmetic thread from the orthogonal (valuation-theoretic) side and would show NO first-order-definable structured set can witness divergence in any of the three natural logics — a definability trichotomy theorem. (3) BOX-BALL: run the integrable-systems playbook on Kari's Collatz-CA: search for local conserved quantities and partial soliton structure in carry streams; a hit = the field's first orbit invariant; a certified miss at all small ranges = a no-go theorem ('the Collatz CA admits no local conservation laws of range ≤ r') that quantifies WHY Collatz resists linearization, in exact parallel to our DFA result. (4) SHADOWING: compute the explicit shadowing modulus of T on Z_2 and prove a transfer lemma 'every length-L window of any hypothetical divergent orbit is 2-adically shadowed by an orbit entering the verified region unless it satisfies explicit congruence rigidity' — potentially converting the 2^71 verification plus Hercher-style bounds into window constraints on divergent orbits. (5) GAMES: compute and classify the SG function of the inverse-Collatz game; automatic P-set feeds our automata infrastructure, non-automatic P-set is a new 'Collatz defeats automata' theorem in a fresh category.
first_move: Candidate 1 (do this first, it is compute-bound and we have 32 cores + solvers): Level-(k,d) certificate search. Build the mod-2^k Collatz transition graph G_k (nodes = residues, edges labeled by the affine branch maps); using SwitchOnSafety.jl / Mosek, solve the SOS feasibility program: find polynomials {V_r}_{r mod 2^k} of degree ≤ d, positive on the cone x ≥ N_0, with V_{T(r)}(T_branch(x)) ≤ λ·V_r(x) + C for each edge, λ < 1, for the accelerated map's per-step growth weighting. Start (k,d) = (8,2) and sweep. The precise first LEMMA to attempt before computing: prove that any homogeneous (C=0, cone-free) certificate is impossible at every finite k because the -1/-5/-17 2-adic cycles embed in G_k with growth 3^m/2^n > 1 — this pins down exactly which affine/cone features a certificate MUST use, and turns every solver failure into interpretable mathematics rather than noise. Parallel cheap moves: Candidate 2 — write out the cell-decomposition reduction of a T-invariant semialgebraic S ⊆ Z_2 to congruence cores (target: 2-page lemma, then invoke Monks); Candidate 3 — implement Kari's base-6 Collatz-CA and run the Hattori–Takesue local-conserved-quantity search for ranges r ≤ 8 (hours on 32 cores); Candidate 5 — SG values of the capped inverse-Collatz game to 10^8 and DFA-learn the P-set with our existing automata tooling.
payoff_odds: Honest per-candidate. (1) Control/JSR: realistic best case = SOS certificate excluding positive divergence (would be a landmark partial result; probability low, ~2-5%, because the certificate must 'see' the difference between N and Z_2\N, which is exactly the hard part — but unlike most approaches, failure is informative); likely case (~60%) = a clean no-go theorem over the analytic certificate class extending our DFA result, publishable in a control or dynamics venue and genuinely clarifying the certificate landscape. This is the best expected-value candidate. (2) p-adic model theory: near-certain (~70%) modest theorem (semialgebraic Monks) within weeks; zero chance it alone resolves anything, but it completes a definability trichotomy with independent interest and the field is moving (two 2026 Büchi papers) — first-mover window is open now. (3) Box-ball: ~90% the conserved-quantity search comes back empty (Collatz-CA should be 'non-integrable'), yielding a small no-go paper; ~5% partial soliton phenomenology in carry streams worth a real paper; <1% a genuine invariant — but that tail outcome would be transformative since no orbit invariant of any kind is known. (4) Shadowing: moderate-low; quantitative lemmas are safe (~80%) but the 2-adic/archimedean mismatch is the known wall — expected value = sharper 'divergent orbits must look locally like verified orbits' window statements, incremental. (5) Games: low; a pretty combinatorics paper (~50%) feeding automata infrastructure; will not touch the conjecture itself. Recommended allocation: 1 > 2 > 3 > 4 > 5.

prior_art.ref: Hung et al., 'Analogy Between the Collatz Conjecture and Sliding Mode Control' (2021)
prior_art.what: Qualitative control-engineering analogy: Collatz as switched linear system with stable (1/2) and unstable (3) branches, reaching/sliding phases; no theorems, no JSR, no Lyapunov synthesis — the bridge noticed but not mined.

prior_art.ref: Leventides–Poulios, 'An operator theoretic approach to the 3x+1 dynamical system' (IFAC-PapersOnLine, 2021)
prior_art.what: Koopman-operator formulation of 3x+1; spectral observations only; no constrained-switching or certificate machinery. Already in our library (leventides-poulios-2010.12987-koopman-3x1.pdf).

prior_art.ref: Blondel–Tsitsiklis, 'The boundedness of all products of a pair of matrices is undecidable' (Syst. Control Lett. 41, 2000) and 'A survey of computational complexity results in systems and control' (Automatica 36, 2000)
prior_art.what: Undecidability of matrix-product boundedness/JSR≤1; the surveys cite the 3x+1 problem as a motivating open problem but prove nothing about it — strongest evidence the control community saw the bridge and left it unmined.

prior_art.ref: Kari, 'Cellular Automata, the Collatz Conjecture and Powers of 3/2' (DLT 2012, LNCS 7410)
prior_art.what: Reversible base-6 CA F_×3 simulating Collatz; pattern-generation results; Mahler Z-numbers as CA orbit question. No integrability, solitons, or conserved-quantity analysis.

prior_art.ref: Kari–Kopra, 'Cellular automata and powers of p/q' (RAIRO-ITA 51, 2017; arXiv:1710.05737)
prior_art.what: Theorems on fractional parts of ξ(p/q)^n via multiplication automata — the only rigorous results on the Collatz-adjacent CA; nothing on invariants of the combined ×3,÷2 dynamics.

prior_art.ref: Monks, 'The sufficiency of arithmetic progressions for the 3x+1 conjecture' (Proc. AMS 134, 2006)
prior_art.what: The Presburger/semilinear rung of the definability ladder: no infinite T-invariant semilinear set avoids {1,2}. Our proposed p-adic semialgebraic theorem is its direct generalization; nobody has done it.

prior_art.ref: Dhiman–Pandey, 'Logical Undefinability of the Generalized Collatz Transition Relation in Büchi Arithmetic' (arXiv:2601.12772, 2026) and 'Non-Definability of Reachability in Büchi Arithmetic for a Family of Generalized Collatz Maps' (arXiv:2602.06066, 2026)
prior_art.what: Brand-new (2026) papers proving the generalized Collatz transition/reachability relations are not Büchi-arithmetic definable via Cobham–Semenov — the automatic-sets rung is being actively mined RIGHT NOW; the semialgebraic rung remains open and the window is closing on adjacent territory.

prior_art.ref: Yolcu–Aaronson–Heule, 'An Automated Approach to the Collatz Conjecture' (CADE 2021; arXiv:2105.14697)
prior_art.what: SAT-searched matrix interpretations for rewriting termination = linear-ranking special case of path-complete Lyapunov certificates; found certificates for weakened variants, none for full Collatz. Defines the baseline our SOS/multinorm search must beat.

prior_art.ref: Rawsthorne?/Carnielli et al., 'Collatz convergence is a Hydra game' (arXiv:2101.09719)
prior_art.what: Casts Collatz termination as a Hydra-style game — ordinal/termination flavor, belongs to our covered 06A/03F lane; NOT combinatorial game theory (no SG values, no misère/loopy analysis). The CGT lane proper contains only a 2010 blog post.

prior_art.ref: Fraenkel, 'The vile, dopey, evil and odious game players' (Discrete Math. 312, 2012); Fraenkel 'Aperiodic subtraction games' (Electron. J. Combin. 18(2), 2011)
prior_art.what: Complete SG analysis of the subtraction-division game Mark (n→n-1, ⌊n/2⌋) via binary digit statistics — the technical template for a Collatz-move game analysis; ×3+1 moves never studied.
